name: drift-viewer
description: Debug SQLite/Drift databases in VS Code with Saropa Drift Advisor.

viewsWelcome.contents:
Saropa Drift Advisor is disabled.

Turn on the extension to discover and connect to Drift debug servers.

[Open Settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22driftViewer.enabled%22%5D)

No Drift debug server connected.

GET STARTED — Add the Drift Advisor package to your project, then run your app with the debug server.

[Add Saropa Drift Advisor](command:driftViewer.addPackageToProject)

[Open in Browser](command:driftViewer.openInBrowser)
[Troubleshooting](command:driftViewer.showTroubleshooting)
[Connection log](command:driftViewer.showConnectionLog)
[Diagnose connection](command:driftViewer.diagnoseConnection)

[Retry Connection](command:driftViewer.retryDiscovery)
[Refresh sidebar UI](command:driftViewer.refreshConnectionUi)
[Forward Port (Android Emulator)](command:driftViewer.forwardPortAndroid)
[Select Server](command:driftViewer.selectServer)

RESOURCES

[Getting Started Guide](https://github.com/saropa/saropa_drift_advisor#getting-started) • [Report Issue](https://github.com/saropa/saropa_drift_advisor/issues)

No Drift debug server connected.

Run your app with the Drift debug server to connect.

[Open in Browser](command:driftViewer.openInBrowser)
[Troubleshooting](command:driftViewer.showTroubleshooting)
[Connection log](command:driftViewer.showConnectionLog)
[Diagnose connection](command:driftViewer.diagnoseConnection)

[Retry Connection](command:driftViewer.retryDiscovery)
[Refresh sidebar UI](command:driftViewer.refreshConnectionUi)
[Forward Port (Android Emulator)](command:driftViewer.forwardPortAndroid)
[Select Server](command:driftViewer.selectServer)

RESOURCES

[Getting Started Guide](https://github.com/saropa/saropa_drift_advisor#getting-started) • [Report Issue](https://github.com/saropa/saropa_drift_advisor/issues)

The sidebar reports a connection (HTTP and/or VM Service), but the **Database** tree could not load schema from the REST API. The embedded browser can still work if it bypasses the same path.

Check `driftViewer.authToken`, host/port, VPN/WSL, and that **Select Server** points at the live process.

[Refresh tree](command:driftViewer.refreshTree)
[Diagnose connection](command:driftViewer.diagnoseConnection)
[Troubleshooting](command:driftViewer.showTroubleshooting)
[Connection log](command:driftViewer.showConnectionLog)
[Open in Browser](command:driftViewer.openInBrowser)
[Select Server](command:driftViewer.selectServer)
[Connection help (web)](command:driftViewer.openConnectionHelp)

commands.title: Import SQL into Visual Query Builder
Scan Dart Schema Definitions (Tables, Columns, Indexes)
Add SQL Query Widget to Dashboard

driftViewer.enabled.description: Master switch: when false, the extension does not discover or connect to Drift debug servers and all Drift Advisor features are off.

driftViewer.authToken.description: Bearer token for authenticating with the Drift debug server.

driftViewer.port.description: Port of the Drift debug server.

driftViewer.host.description: Host of the Drift debug server.

driftViewer.connection.logEveryUiRefresh.description: Log every connection UI refresh even when state is unchanged (verbose; for deep troubleshooting).

driftViewer.discovery.enabled.description: Automatically scan for running Drift debug servers.

driftViewer.logVerbosity.description: Controls how much runtime information the extension writes to Output → Saropa Drift Advisor channels.

driftViewer.discovery.portRangeStart.description: Start of port range to scan for Drift debug servers.

driftViewer.discovery.portRangeEnd.description: End of port range to scan for Drift debug servers (inclusive).

driftViewer.database.loadOnConnect.description: When true, load the Database tree as soon as a server connects. When false, load on first time the Database view is shown.

driftViewer.database.allowOfflineSchema.description: When the debug server is unreachable, repopulate the Database tree from the last-known persisted schema for this workspace when available.

driftViewer.dartSchemaScan.openOutput.description: When running **Scan Dart schema definitions**, open the Drift Dart schema output channel automatically.

driftViewer.schemaCache.ttlMs.description: Schema cache TTL in ms. Cached schema is reused for this long before refetch. 0 disables in-memory cache.

driftViewer.schemaCache.persistKey.description: Workspace state key for last-known schema (stale-while-revalidate on startup). Empty disables persist.

driftViewer.lightweight.description: When true, disables file badges, timeline auto-capture, and tree refresh on generation change to reduce load.

driftViewer.tasks.blockOnWarnings.description: Block app launch when health check finds warnings (not just errors).

driftViewer.performance.slowThresholdMs.description: Queries slower than this (ms) are highlighted as slow.

driftViewer.performance.refreshIntervalM.description: How often to refresh performance data during debug (ms).

driftViewer.performance.logToCapture.description: Log queries to Saropa Log Capture: off, slow queries only, or all queries.

driftViewer.integrations.includeInLogCap.description: How much to send to Saropa Log Capture at session end: none, header only, or full (header + meta + sidecar).

driftViewer.perfRegression.enabled.description: Detect query performance regressions when a debug session ends.

driftViewer.perfRegression.threshold.description: Regression alert threshold: current avg / baseline avg. E.g. 2.0 means 2x slower.

driftViewer.perfRegression.recordBaselin.description: When the Query Replay DVR panel loads or refreshes, merge each timeline row's duration into the perf baseline store (same normalized SQL keys as session baselines).

driftViewer.perfRegression.warnOnDvrPane.description: When true, each DVR panel refresh compares DVR timings to baselines and may show a regression warning (off by default — can be noisy).

driftViewer.fileBadges.enabled.description: Show row count badges on Drift table files in the explorer.

driftViewer.hover.enabled.description: Show database row preview on hover during debug sessions.

driftViewer.hover.maxRows.description: Number of recent rows to show in hover preview.

driftViewer.linter.enabled.description: Show database diagnostics on Drift table definitions.

driftViewer.linter.anomalySeverity.description: Severity level for data anomaly diagnostics.

driftViewer.timeline.maxSnapshots.description: Maximum number of snapshots to keep in memory.

driftViewer.timeline.autoCapture.description: Automatically capture snapshots when database data changes.

driftViewer.timeline.minIntervalMs.description: Minimum milliseconds between automatic snapshot captures.

driftViewer.watch.notifications.description: Show desktop notifications when watched data changes.

driftViewer.watch.maxWatchers.description: Maximum number of simultaneous watch queries.

driftViewer.dataBreakpoints.enabled.description: Enable data breakpoint evaluation during debug sessions.

driftViewer.fkHyperlinks.enabled.description: Show foreign key values as clickable links in table data views.

driftViewer.impact.maxDepth.description: Maximum FK traversal depth for row impact analysis.

driftViewer.impact.maxRowsPerTable.description: Maximum rows to fetch per table branch in impact analysis.

driftViewer.seeder.defaultRowCount.description: Default number of rows to generate per table.

driftViewer.seeder.nullProbability.description: Probability of generating NULL for nullable columns (0.0-1.0).

driftViewer.sqlNotebook.maxHistory.description: Maximum number of SQL queries to keep in history.

driftViewer.nlSql.apiUrl.description: LLM API endpoint for NL-to-SQL (OpenAI-compatible chat completions).

driftViewer.nlSql.model.description: LLM model used for NL-to-SQL generation.

driftViewer.nlSql.maxTokens.description: Maximum response tokens for NL-to-SQL generation.

driftViewer.nlSql.seedSuggestions.description: When true, NL-to-SQL shows a QuickPick of past questions, saved filters, and notebook SQL history before the LLM runs.

driftViewer.nlSql.maxSchemaTables.description: Max tables included in the NL-to-SQL schema prompt; extra tables are listed as omitted to control token size.

driftViewer.nlSql.maxSchemaContextChars.description: Hard cap on total schema context characters sent to the LLM; the builder truncates with a trailing notice when exceeded.

driftViewer.invariants.continuous.description: Re-evaluate invariants on every database change (may impact performance).

driftViewer.invariants.defaultSeverity.description: Default severity level for new invariant violations.

driftViewer.report.defaultMaxRows.description: Default maximum rows per table in exported portable reports.

driftViewer.report.includeSchema.description: Include schema SQL (CREATE TABLE statements) in exported reports.

driftViewer.report.includeAnomalies.description: Include anomaly report in exported reports.

driftViewer.diagnostics.tableExclusions.description: Suppress specific diagnostic rules on specific tables. Keys are rule codes, values are arrays of SQL table names.

driftViewer.dashboard.showOnConnect.description: Show a notification to open the Dashboard when a server first connects.

driftViewer.dvr.autoRecord.description: Automatically start Query Replay DVR recording when a Dart/Flutter debug session begins.

driftViewer.dvr.maxQueries.description: Maximum number of DVR query events retained by the server ring buffer.

driftViewer.dvr.captureBeforeAfter.description: Request before/after state capture for write queries when supported by the server.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Saropa Drift Advisor
walkthroughs.description: Learn how to connect to your Flutter/Drift app and explore your SQLite database.

steps.title: Add the Drift Advisor Package
steps.description:
Add `saropa_drift_advisor` to your Flutter project's dependencies. This enables the debug server that the extension connects to.

[Add Saropa Drift Advisor](command:driftViewer.addPackageToProject)

media.altText: Adding the drift advisor package to your project

steps.title: Connect to a Running App
steps.description:
Start your Flutter app in debug mode. The extension auto-discovers Drift debug servers, or connects via VM Service during debugging.

Check the status bar for connection state. If offline, click it to retry.

[Retry Discovery](command:driftViewer.retryDiscovery)
[Select Server](command:driftViewer.selectServer)

steps.title: Explore Your Database
steps.description:
Open the Database Explorer in the sidebar to see your tables, columns, and foreign keys. Click any table to view its data.

[Open Database Explorer](command:driftViewer.databaseExplorer.focus)

steps.title: Run a Health Check
steps.description:
Get an instant assessment of your database's health — index coverage, FK integrity, null density, query performance, and more.

[Run Health Check](command:driftViewer.healthScore)

steps.title: Generate a Migration
steps.description:
Diff your Dart code schema against the runtime database and generate migration code automatically.

[Schema Diff](command:driftViewer.schemaDiff)
[Generate Migration](command:driftViewer.generateMigration)
